message:
fix(messaging): narrow Kafka mutex scopes, refactor Redis PubSub parsing, add NATS hardening tests

Kafka consumer:
- Narrow lock scope in poll(), commit_offsets(), and seek() to avoid
  holding the mutex across validation logic that may return early. This
  separates read-only subscription checks from mutable state updates,
  preventing potential deadlocks and unnecessary lock contention.

Redis:
- Change expect_ok_response to take &RespValue (borrow) instead of
  consuming by value, avoiding unnecessary clones at call sites.
- Remove unused imports (RedisPubSub, Transaction) now defined in-file.
- Extract monolithic parse_event match arms into dedicated helpers:
  parse_message_event, parse_pmessage_event, parse_subscription_event,
  parse_pong_event, with shared next_required/ensure_no_trailing utils.
  Makes pubsub kind matching case-insensitive for subscription events.
- Add tests for expect_ok_response and all PubSub event parse paths.

NATS:
- Add T6.7 hardening tests covering MAX_READ_BUFFER enforcement,
  oversized buffer rejection, max_payload config/parsing, NATS token
  validation (including \\r\\n injection and tab rejection), JSON escape
  helpers, and IPv6 URL parsing edge cases.

Also updates e2e_messaging.rs to follow the ConsumerConfig ->
KafkaConsumerConfig rename.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
